query.sentence: Identify PFAS free thermoplastic material for heat shrink tubing with a 2:1 shrink ratio that could replace FEP heat shrink tubing that has an application / service temperature of up to ~200°C and a shrink temperature under 250°C.

properties: Good mechanical strength and dimensional stability during shrinkage
Chemical resistance suitable for typical heat‑shrink applications
constraints: Must be free of per‑ and polyfluoroalkyl substances (no fluoropolymers or fluoroelastomers)
Shrink temperature must not exceed 250 °C to avoid degradation of surrounding components
Material must be processable into tubing by extrusion or similar lab‑scale methods

final_candidate.justification: PAI (polyamide‑imide) is a PFAS‑free, high‑performance thermoplastic with a service temperature range up to 260 °C and excellent dimensional stability. Commercially available PAI grades such as PA6/66 can be extruded into heat‑shrink tubing that achieves a 2:1 shrink ratio when heated to ~210–220 °C. The polymer’s high glass transition temperature (~200 °C) allows the tube to retain its shape during handling and application, while the melt viscosity at extrusion temperatures (≈280–300 °C) permits smooth tube formation. PAI also offers good mechanical strength, low moisture absorption, and chemical resistance comparable to fluoropolymers, making it a suitable replacement for FEP in high‑temperature heat‑shrink applications.

process_recipe.description: Dry PAI pellets to remove moisture (≤10 °C, 30 min).
Extrude PAI into tube shape using a single‑die extruder.
process_recipe.conditions: Melt temperature 280–300 °C, screw speed 50 rpm, die pressure 5 bar.

process_recipe.description: Cool the extruded tube in a water bath to solidify and set dimensions.
Cut the tube to desired length (e.g., 50 mm) and pre‑shrink by heating to 200 °C for 30 s.
process_recipe.conditions: Convection oven, ramp rate 10 °C/s, hold at 200 °C for 30 s.

process_recipe.description: Cool the pre‑shrink tube to room temperature and test shrink ratio by applying a heat source (e.g., 220 °C hot plate) for 10 s.
